name: Container and Trailer Nominal Volumes
description: Nominal internal volumes for common ocean containers and a 53ft US trailer, used by the CBM calculator's container-fit estimate. NOT in the knowledge base. Candidate values below are common industry figures and MUST be confirmed against ISO 668 / carrier equipment guides before being treated as verified.
review_frequency: yearly (container specs are stable; carrier equipment guides occasionally revise usable-volume figures)
source: TO CONFIRM: ISO 668 series 1 freight container dimensions; ocean-carrier equipment guides (e.g. Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd container specifications); trailer OEM specs for the 53ft dry van.
sourcing_notes: Values are nominal internal capacities commonly quoted in equipment guides (20ft DV ~33 m3, 40ft DV ~67 m3, 40ft HC ~76 m3, 53ft trailer ~114 m3). Real-world loadable volume is lower — practical loading typically achieves roughly 80-90% of nominal capacity depending on carton sizes, palletisation, and stacking rules — so the calculator presents fill % as an estimate only. Confirm each value against ISO 668 and a current carrier equipment guide, record the source per row, and set status to verified once confirmed.
